entry.title: how can I use a cursor on an initially empty, growing table?
entry.content:
I want to walk thru an initially empty table that is filled while doing this. So I constantly add rows to that table while another cursor should catch up. If the catching up cursor reaches the end, the process should stop. Here s some pseudcode in case that wasn't clear: 

:MyTab is an empty table
:ResultTab is an empty table, that I want to walk
:currentValue is something I fetched, starts with a given value
DO {
  EXECUTE IMMEDIATE (something with :currentValue) INTO :MyTab
  /* I might get from 0 to whatever results. */
  Append :MyTab to :ResultTable
  Fetch next :currentValue from :ResultTable
} WHILE (:currentValue is NOT NULL)

 My Problem is: how can I do this? If I declare a cursor, I have to open it outside the loop on the empty table, and will get an invalid result. I tried to play with HOLD but that didnt work. Is it safe to use "$rowid$" here on :ResultTable and just increment?

How would you do it?

entry.title: Issues with Extract Data Template in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,
We have been facing problems with the Extract Data (Template) activity in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA). The document extraction process is failing consistently, resulting in timeout errors and preventing successful data retrieval.

Observed Errors:

  • When running the Extract Data (Template) step, the process keeps running without extracting any data.

  • After 300 seconds, the workflow fails with a timeout error: “No extraction result could be retrieved from Document Information Extraction service after 300 seconds.”

  • When attempting to add a new document template, the system does not show a preview of the uploaded document.

  • Instead, we encounter a “Document upload failed” error, which blocks template editing and preview functionality.

    I am attaching the required screenshots:

    Has anyone else faced this issue, and is there a recommended solution? Any guidance on how to resolve the Extract Data (Template) timeout and the document upload failed error in SAP BPA would be greatly appreciated.

    Thanks in advance!

    SAP Build Process Automation 

    @HristoSimeonov 
    @Yogananda 
    @25shahindrajit25 
    @Dan_Wroblewski 
    @maria_kondratyeva 
    @BorisZarske

entry.title: Autonomous Enterprise beginnt im Betrieb der Software
entry.content:
Die Adoption neuer Technologien und echte Business Transformation setzen eines voraus: einen Software-Betrieb, der die Veränderungen trägt. Denn KI-Szenarien, neue Prozesse und Innovationen entfalten nur dann Wirkung, wenn das operative Fundament stabil, transparent und automatisiert ist.

Warum das gerade jetzt relevant ist

Die KI-Fähigkeiten in SAP-Landschaften wachsen rasant - von Joule als KI-Copilot über intelligente Automatisierung in Geschäftsprozessen bis hin zu zunehmend autonomen, KI-gestützten Funktionen in SAP-Lösungen.

Doch KI anzuwenden heißt nicht, sie einfach „einzuschalten“. Es erfordert Setup, Konfiguration, Anbindung, Monitoring und operative Governance.

Kurz gesagt: SAP Cloud Application Services  unterstützen Unternehmen dabei, KI-Funktionen nicht nur einzuführen, sondern zuverlässig zu operationalisieren - je nach vereinbartem Serviceumfang.

Was SAP Cloud Application Services konkret ermöglichen

  • Fokus auf Geschäftsergebnisse: SAP unterstützt operative Aufgaben rund um Administration, Monitoring und Governance von KI-Funktionen.
  • Stabiler Betrieb: Unterstützung für einen sicheren und skalierbaren Betrieb von KI-Funktionen.
  • Schnellere Adoption: Joule und intelligente Services lassen sich schneller nutzbar machen - mit kürzerer Time-to-Value.
  • Weniger manuelle Fehler: KI-gestützte Ausführung und Insights schaffen mehr Transparenz.
  • Kosteneffizienz: Zugriff auf SAPs globalen Expertenpool statt Aufbau eigener KI-Ops-Teams.
  • Security & Datenschutz: Unterstützung bei Datenschutz- und Sicherheitsanforderungen in KI-Szenarien.

KI liefert nur dann Mehrwert, wenn sie operationalisiert ist. SAP Cloud Application Services schaffen dafür die Grundlage.

Praxisbeispiel: SMA Solar Technology

Wie das in der Praxis aussieht, zeigen Dr. Arndt-Alexander Boehnert, Global Head & VP Cloud Application Services Sales, und Gerhild Muschet, Senior Solution Advisor, in einem Live-Interview am 23. Juni - unter anderem am Beispiel von SMA Solar Technology.

„Der Einsatz privater und öffentlicher Cloud-Lösungen von SAP sowie SAP Cloud Application Services ermöglicht es SMA Solar, sich auf die Weiterentwicklung des Kerngeschäfts und die digitale Transformation zu konzentrieren.

Durch die Entlastung unseres Teams von Routineaufgaben und den Zugang zu SAP-Experten können wir Innovationen schneller umsetzen und flexibel auf Marktveränderungen reagieren. Eine vertrauensvolle Partnerschaft und klare Governance-Strukturen sind entscheidende Erfolgsfaktoren.“

Heiko Kehm
Head of Digital Supply Chain, SMA Solar Technology AG

In der Session erfahrt Ihr

  • Wie ihr ein belastbares Betriebsfundament für KI-Szenarien schafft
  • Welche Pain Points Unternehmen heute erleben - und wie SAP Cloud Application Services über klassische AMS-Modelle hinaus unterstützen
  • Wie der Weg von Discovery bis zum produktiven Start aussehen kann, inklusive Live-Demo und Q&A mit den Experten

Wann? Am 23. Juni 2026, 10:00-10:30 Uhr. Wer Interesse hat, kann sich hier anmelden. Wir freuen uns auf Euch!

entry.title: Re: Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Organization
entry.content:
Response from SAP: 

 

For JIT call processing, in the “Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Organization” configuration, it is not required to maintain LZJ in the Sales Order Type field.
 
You can either maintain it as OR to process the sales order via API and continue with 2EL process.

entry.title: Re: Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Organization
entry.content:
Response from SAP:

For JIT call processing, in the “Assign Customer/Supplier to Sales Organization” configuration, it is not required to maintain LZJ in the Sales Order Type field.

You can either maintain it as OR to process the sales order via API and continue with 2EL process.

entry.title: Software Monetization: End-to-End Automation with SAP’s "Subscription Management for Software"
entry.content:
In the modern SaaS and software landscape, providing a seamless customer experience requires more than just a slick user interface. True operational excellence lies under the hood: How efficiently an organization handles everything from initial bundle configuration and automated tenant provisioning to consumption tracking, billing, and complex revenue recognition.

In this article, we will walk through an integrated, end-to-end architecture built on SAP Scope Item 5SW – “Subscription Management for Software”. We will look at how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Subscription Billing, and SAP Entitlement Management seamlessly support an integrated and automated quote-to-cash cycle for software subscriptions, and how we can extend this flow using SAP Universal Revenue Recognition to effortlessly handle complex, multi-element revenue splits.

Our real-world scenario follows a customer purchasing a 12-month contract for a CAD Software "Bronze" Edition, where the backend financial engine must split the incoming revenue between two distinct internal software divisions: CAD and Collaboration.

 

Integrated Software Monetization in Action: Step-by-Step

Configuring the Solution Order & Price Modeling

The journey begins in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, where a sales representative configures a CAD bundle within a Solution Order.

For our example, the customer selects the CAD Bundle Edition "Bronze" for a 12-month term, with a capacity of 20 users. To handle the subsequent financial allocation, the system automatically explodes the bundle into three operational sub-items:

  • MZ-CAD-BRONZE (The Commercial Header): Carries the actual recurring contract price of $100 per user/month, totaling $2,000/month. For clean revenue accounting, its individual Standalone Selling Price (SSP) is set to 0.
  • MZ-CAD & MZ-COLLAB (The Revenue Split Items): These sub-items carry a recurring price of $0 for billing purposes but hold the Standalone Selling Prices (SSP) of 80 and 20, respectively. This establishes the required 80:20 revenue split.

 

 

 

Behind the Tech: To ensure dynamic pricing, the system evaluates the exact software edition during configuration. While the Bronze and Silver Edition dictate an 80:20 split between CAD and Collaboration, a Gold Edition might trigger a 60:20:20 split since it also includes Analytics. The configuration data is cleanly passed from Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) down to the sub-items using custom fields and Business Add-Ins (BAdIs), ensuring perfect replication to the SAP Subscription Billing pricing and billing logic.

 

 

 

 

 

Orchestrating Contracts and Entitlements

Once the Solution Order is released, automation takes over. SAP Subscription Billing instantly generates three corresponding subscription contracts. At this stage, the contract for the CAD Bronze Edition has a Pending status because the actual software infrastructure hasn't been delivered yet.

 

 

 

Simultaneously, the contract payload initializes records in SAP Entitlement Management. Based on the "Bronze" edition mapping, the system generates:

  • Entitlements for productive and test tenants (CAD and Collaboration).
  • User, document licenses and licenses for core capabilities like CAD 2D Essentials and Collaboration Basics, included in the Bronze Edition.

These entitlements enter the world as In Preparation, waiting for the physical provisioning loop.

 

The Provisioning Loop: Bridging SAP quote-to-cash and Cloud Ops

When entitlements hit the system, SAP Entitlement Management dispatches an outbound provisioning message to the external software delivery or cloud orchestration platform.

Once the cloud infrastructure builds the environment, it returns an asynchronous API feedback payload. This message delivers vital cloud telemetry back to SAP—specifically the unique Tenant ID, Tenant URL, and Tenant Name.

 

Upon receiving this feedback:

1. Provisioning is completed, and the tenant entitlement record updates instantly and shifts to Active.

 

 

2. Once all dependent entitlements for the subscription are also active, SAP Entitlement Management shoots a webhook notification back to SAP Subscription Billing.

 

3. The contract status in SAP Subscription Billing automatically moves from Pending to Active, unlocking the billing lifecycle.

 

 

Tracking Value: Compliance and Software Adoption

Once a customer is live, the ecosystem shifts from delivery to monitoring. SAP Entitlement Management effectively serves as a dual-layer data hub:

License Compliance

As part of the monthly cycle, aggregated usage data can be piped into SAP via event platforms like SAP Convergent Mediation. For example, if a customer licensed 20 user seats but a monthly check-in shows only 10 are actively consumed as deployed users in the tenant, the system flags 10 remaining activations. This helps ensuring software compliance.

 

Software Adoption (Usage Insights)

Beyond contractual compliance, the platform tracks granular adoption metrics across both test and productive tenants. By capturing application and capability-level data—such as active users, document counts, or specific capability/feature utilization—businesses can easily distinguish between an active implementation, live production usage, or a stalled deployment. This deep telemetry provides Product Management and Customer Success teams with the clear visibility needed to proactively mitigate churn risks and uncover expansion up-selling opportunities as customers approach their entitlement limits.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Closing the Financial Loop: Invoice to Revenue Recognition

At the end of each billing period, SAP Subscription Billing creates a Billing Document Request (BDR), which S/4HANA Cloud transforms into a customer invoice. The invoice cleanly displays the line item for the $2,000 monthly subscription along with the operational sub-items.

 

 

 

 

This is where SAP Universal Revenue Recognition steps in to handle the final financial magic:

  • The Revenue Contract: The system automatically establishes a revenue contract tying the sub-items together over the 12-month horizon, tracking a Total Contract Value of $24,000 ($2,000 x 12 months).
  • The Performance Obligations (POBs): Universal Revenue Recognition recognizes the 80:20 Standalone Selling Price (SSP) weighting.
  • The Automated Ledger Posting: When the periodic revenue recognition job executes, it automatically posts 80% of the recognized revenue into the CAD Software Division and 20% into the Collaboration Division.

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion: The Power of SAP’s Integrated Software Monetization Platform

For modern software providers, manual cross-system reconciliation is the enemy of growth. By unifying S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Subscription Billing, and SAP Entitlement Management, organizations can achieve a highly automated, touchless quote-to-cash process for software subscriptions.

From modeling complex, multi-element SaaS bundles to automating cloud provisioning, tracking real-world telemetry, and perfectly executing billing and revenue recognition, this architecture eliminates revenue leakage and operational friction. The result? A scalable foundation designed to accelerate recurring revenue growth for software subscriptions.

You have questions about setting up this integrated software end-to-end flow? Let's connect in the comments below!

Additional Resources

  • Summary Blog on SAP Entitlement Management
  • Scope item 5SW "Subscription Management for Software" - SAP best practices template for software E2E process with Cloud ERP, SAP Subscription Billing, SAP Entitlement Management - see detailed webinars below
  • Customer Testimonial Video Honeywell: Connecting End-to-End Software Processes with SAP
  • More details on SAP Entitlement Management, learn how Roche and ServiceNow have implemented software subscription end to end processes with SAP 
  • SAP Managed Tag: SAP Entitlement Management

entry.title: Re: Serial Number modification - Production Order - S/4HA...
entry.content:
Hi,

Could you clarify your question? Are you saying the standard logic is not sufficient to determine serial numbers based on your desired rules?

Here is the help for serial numbers in production orders: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/0c05b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

"

For each material, you can specify the following:

  • When the serial number is assigned. For example, when the production order is created or during goods receipt.

  • Whether serial numbers must be assigned at that point in time, whether the user can decide if serial numbers must be assigned, or whether the serial numbers are assigned automatically by the system"

If the standard logic is not sufficient for you, you can look at doing it with the API and do the serial number assignment with extensibility:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/f3e3ee2158894ffd811ef2eedbe91f5c.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

There are events for Production Order creation and change you could leverage: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/1d609d0a1ecb48c7b46831a3d473288f.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Finally, you can consider to use On Stack Developer Extensibility https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_PRODUCTIONORDERTP with the available objects:

 

I hope this helps!

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Serial Number modification - Production Order - S/4HA...
entry.content:
Dear @16175219,

In Public Cloud serial number creation for production orders is controlled through the Serial Number Profile assigned to the material. For production order scenarios the relevant standard serialization procedures are PPAU/PPRL which are used for serial number handling in production orders. SAP confirms this scenario for Public Cloud and also confirms that serial numbers required for production must be maintained in the production order context, not entered only later during goods receipt. If serial numbers are entered only in the goods receipt material document they are not passed back to the production order

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3563088

The serial number profile is configured in SSCUI 103636 Define Serial Number Profiles.

Below SAP KBA if there are access problems:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3228491

If the standard internal numbering is not sufficient and the customer needs serial numbers to be generated according to specific business rules the supported clean-core option is to use SAP released extensibility for serial number creation. SAP provides the Extensibility for Serial Number Creation framework, which allows serial numbers to be created automatically according to a custom pattern when automatic serial number creation is triggered. The relevant options are:

  • Key-user extensibility: LOSN_CBO_SERIAL_NUMBER_INT
  • Developer extensibility: LOSN_SER_NUM_INT_CREATE

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/20f4d8b2420544f7a5e41471c5bf5dac.html

SAP also provides an enhancement to use production version data during internal serial number creation, which can be relevant if the business rule depends on the production version

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/206de6162bd6444d8c9d5e843d825cd2.html

Recommended approach:

1. Verify that the finished product has the correct Serial Number Profile assigned

2. Verify the profile configuration in SSCUI 103636 Define Serial Number Profiles, including the production-related procedures such as PPAU/PPRL. PPRL controls serial number handling at production order release. If the requirement is automatic serial number creation at release, configure PPRL for automatic serial number assignment/creation according to the available value help in the SSCUI

 

3. If serial numbers must be generated automatically according to customer-specific rules, use SAP released serial number creation extensibility when automatic internal serial number creation is triggered

 

4. Implement the required numbering rule through the released serial number creation BAdI:

    • LOSN_CBO_SERIAL_NUMBER_INT for key-user extensibility
    • LOSN_SER_NUM_INT_CREATE for developer extensibility
    • Both options are intended for automatic internal serial number creation. The developer extensibility option should be used when the logic is more complex and requires development in the 3-system landscape

5. Test the process end to end

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Need to avoid defaulting company code in business par...
entry.content:
This is standard SAP behavior, not a bug. SAP assumes that most vendors are first created for the leading / default company code and therefore pre-fills it to speed up master data creation.

In S/4HANA, vendors are technically synchronized via CVI.
CVI requires a leading company code to propose FI data.
If company code 1000 is:
First created in the system
Marked as leading
Used during initial CVI activation
SAP treats 1000 as the “default / fallback company code”

entry.title: Blocking Modification of purchase info record
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team

We need to modify the prices in the app Change Purshasing Info Record.

In the Condition section, we need to modify the price without creating another condition period.

The price field is locked, it does not allow you to change the field to modify the price.

We don't want to add another period, we need to directly modify the price.

Thank you in advance.

Greetings

entry.title: Re: how can I use a cursor on an initially empty, growing...
entry.content:
I'm not sure if this is a good way to do it (I'm a newbee to SQL, especially HANA), but this is how got this to work:

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE "q_FollowLinkedTables"
/**
 * List BaseEntries and TargetEntries of a given B1 Entry/Type combination
 * V2.2 Coded 2026-05-20 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
  IN  EntryNumber int,
  IN  EntryType   int,
  IN  Backwards   BOOLEAN,
  OUT Result      "q_tt_EntryMap"
)
/* type q_tt_EntryMap is a table (Entry,Type) */
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
  LineTable     NVARCHAR(10);
  tSQL          NVARCHAR(4000);
  ActualTab     "q_tt_EntryMap";
  AllTab        "q_tt_EntryMap";
  TypeField     NVARCHAR(10);
  NumberField   NVARCHAR(10);
  counter       int;
  CURSOR curPos FOR SELECT * FROM :ActualTab;
BEGIN

  counter := 0;
  IF :Backwards = TRUE THEN
    TypeField    := 'BaseType';
    NumberField  := 'BaseEntry';
  ELSE
    TypeField    := 'TargetType';
    NumberField  := 'TrgetEntry';
  END IF;
  /* This fn returns "INV" for 13 and so on */
  LineTable    := "q_GetObjTable"(EntryType) || '1';
  
  WHILE :LineTable IS NOT NULL
  DO
    tSQL := '
    SELECT DISTINCT "'|| :NumberField ||'" AS "Entry", "'|| :TypeField ||'" AS "Type"
    FROM ' || :LineTable || '
    WHERE "DocEntry" = ' || :EntryNumber || ' AND "'|| :TypeField ||'" <> -1
    ';
    EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL INTO ActualTab;
    /* here I push the (perhaps multiple) results into AllTab */
    FOR entry AS curPos
    DO
      INSERT INTO :AllTab VALUES (entry."Entry", entry."Type");
    END FOR;

    /* here I get the next value at counter's pos from  */
    SELECT "Entry" ,"Type"
    INTO EntryNumber, EntryType DEFAULT NULL,NULL
    FROM :AllTab
    LIMIT 1 OFFSET :counter;

    counter := counter + 1;
    
    LineTable := "q_GetObjTable"(EntryType) || '1';
    
    /* reasonable safeguard to prevent recursive locks */
    IF :counter > 15 THEN
      BREAK;
    END IF;

  END WHILE;

  Result = SELECT * FROM :AllTab;

END;

Hi @ChristianSilva ,

 

its all about exposure of SAP functionality to external applications (MCP clients)

In that case you are not interacting with LLM directly on ABAP stack - during which you can refer to tools / functions - which can be used by LLM so you are using its response to call up e.g. class methods dynamically.

For instance, you have a custom development implemented in ABAP and you are not (only) expose an API (OData / REST) with which you can work with your business object from an external system, but you are exposing your agents configured in yaai so your custom development / business object acts as MCP Server.

This is just an idea, maybe I overlook or mix up things 🙂

entry.title: Launch Transactions from Web UI if the Browser Version is NOT Compatible with the SAP Version
entry.content:
Background:

The GUI Transaction getting launched from Web UI via Transaction Launcher might throw an error "500 Internal Server Error" and not get launched properly.

On analysis, it was found that this was due to Non-Compatibility between the SAP Version of the Target system and the Internet Browser Version.

In this blog, I have explained how to resolve the error and launch the SAP GUI Transactions in case you get error due to incompatibility with browser version.

Analysis of the Error: 

  •  On analysis, it was found that this was due to Non-Compatibility between the SAP Version of the Target system and the Internet Browser Version.
  • To find the reason for the error, I went to F12->Developer tools -> Emulation Tab and checked the Browser Version. It was IE5. On selecting the Browser Version to IE9 from the dropdown the GUI Transaction in the Target system got launched properly.
  • Based on this it was Concluded that Non-Compatibility between SAP Target system Version and Browser version is the reason for this Error.
    • Suggested Solutions:
    • If we resolve by upgrading the browser version to IE9 it would involve lot of Impact analysis across all SAP and Non-SAP system and the solution is very time consuming.
    • To resolve this error, we can either upgrade the browser Version to IE9 (or) launch the ECC Transaction in the Target system using JavaScript. Open approach.
    • If we resolve by upgrading the browser version to IE9 it would involve lot of Impact analysis across all SAP and Non-SAP system and the solution to be ready for End User was very time consuming.
    • Hence the second approach to launch the Transaction using javascript.open approach was followed.
    • Steps for the Solution:
    • In TCode se11 build a Custom Database table to hold the Parameter name, System name and the date from which the New Logic must be active. Build a Table Maintenance generator for this table.
    • Then go to TCode BSP_WD_CMPWB and check if the Component CRMCMP_NAVBAR is enhanced. If it is not enhanced, then enhance the component.
    • Enhance the View ShortCutLinks.
    • Go to the.htm page and check if the current date sy-datum is greater than the date from which the New Logic must be triggered.
    • If yes, then the New Logic will be triggered. If not, the New Logic will not be triggered.
    • Call the method get_shortcuttable and get the values of the NavBar Profile, Work Center, Work Center group links, Logic links into an Internal table lt_dlgs (from Internal table ct_dlgs ).
    • Create a Custom class in se24 having four methods to generate the URL at runtime.
    • The Four Custom methods are:
      • ModifyShortcut
      • GetURL_BY_IDS
      • BuildURLS
      • BuildJS4URLS

      •  Method ModifyShortcut:  

      • In this method, I first read the value of the logical link and pass the relevant values to the next method GET_URL_BY_ID.

      • Method GetURL_BY_IDS:

      • In this method, I got the Protocol, URL, Logical system by selecting from the database table CRMC_IC_LTX_URL and CRMC_IC_LTX_URLT. If the Protocol is http, I assigned http to a variable. If the protocol is https, I assigned https to a variable.

      • Method BuildURLS:

      • In this method I got the RFC destination of the remote system by calling the method GET_RFCDEST_FROM_LOGSYS of the class CL_CRM_IC_LTX_REMOTECONBASE. Then I used the method create_url of the class cl_crm_web_utility and passed the Protocol, RFC Destination, generated the URL and assigned to a Variable rv_result.

      •  Then I assigned ‘WINDOW’ to another Variable lv_window.

      •  Then I called the method BUILDJS4URL by passing the variable rv_result and lv_window.

      • Method BuildJS4URLS:

      • In this method I assigned the string give below to a variable lv_link.

        lv_link  = Javascript:window.Open (&&rv_result&&, lv_window , width, height, resizable =1, scrollbars = 1).

      •  This variable lv_link generated in the method BuildJs4URL was assigned to the parameter ONCLIENTCLICK in the Internal table lt_dlgs of the method Modifyshortcuts .

      • By this when the User tries to launch the ECC transaction the Component CRMCMP_NAVBAR and View Getshortlinks get triggered. In the .htm page the validation of the current date is greater than (or) equal to the date in the Custom table is done and if yes, then the method get_shortcuttable is called and the custom class methods are called and the url is generated and assigned to the Parameter ONCLIENTCLICK.

        By following the above approach, I was able to launch GUI Transaction without having to change the browser version.

                                            *End of Document*

entry.title: Guia para acessar o suporte de SAP Concur
entry.content:
Saber exatamente qual caminho seguir para solicitar suporte faz toda a diferença na velocidade da solução. Abaixo, você encontra o direcionamento correto de acordo com o seu perfil dentro do SAP Concur.

  • Para Administradores e Authorized Support Contacts (ASC) 

Se você é Administrador do SAP Concur ou um Authorized Support Contact (ASC), seu caminho oficial para suporte é por meio do portal da SAP. 

O acesso deve ser feito pelo SAP for Me. 

Siga os passos: 

  1. Acesse: https://me.sap.com/ 
  2. Faça login com seu S-User ID e senha 
    (Seu Super Aministrador pode criar S-Users e atribuir as autorizações necessárias.) 
  3. No dashboard Services & Support, clique em Get Support (Case, Expert Chat…) 
  4. Siga o fluxo guiado para abertura do chamado. 

Durante a criação do caso: 

  • O sistema pode sugerir soluções automaticamente em tempo real 
  • Sua solicitação pode ser direcionada para o canal mais adequado, incluindo Expert Chat 
  • Todas as questões técnicas serão tratadas exclusivamente por meio do chamado registrado 

Administradores também contam com acesso ao SAP Concur Support Portal, além de materiais avançados de configuração e gestão. 

 

  • Para Usuários Finais (Colaboradores, Solicitantes de Despesas ou Viagens) 

Se você utiliza o SAP Concur para registrar despesas, reservar viagens ou enviar faturas e não possui um S-User ID, o caminho ideal depende do modelo de suporte contratado pela sua empresa.  

Primeiros passos: 

  1. Faça login no SAP Concur 
  2. Clique no ícone de (?)  Ajuda ou selecione “Contact Support” na página inicial 

O que acontece depois? 

  • Se sua empresa tiver o serviço User Support Desk (USD), você será direcionado ao suporte técnico de primeiro nível. 
  • Caso o User Support Desk não esteja disponível, o atendimento será feito pelo suporte interno da sua empresa (geralmente times de Financeiro, RH, Contabilidade ou TI). 

Dica importante: você pode encontrar os administradores da sua empresa na seção Company Notes dentro do sistema. 

 

  • Recursos online que podem ajudar 

Antes mesmo de abrir um chamado, você pode encontrar mais informações sobre o Suporte Concur aqui. Alternativamente, você também pode acessar a SAP Concur Community. 

  

  • Por que seguir o caminho correto faz diferença? 

Quando você utiliza o canal adequado para o seu perfil: 

  • Sua solicitação é direcionada imediatamente ao time certo 
  • O tempo de resposta é reduzido 
  • A experiência se torna mais fluida e eficiente 

Esperamos que este guia torne o processo de contato com o suporte do SAP Concur mais claro e simples para você. 

Se você já utilizou esses canais, compartilhe sua experiência com sua equipe e continue acompanhando nosso conteúdo para mais orientações, boas práticas e novidades sobre o suporte da SAP.

entry.title: Re: Regarding Retention Period Limit in SAP Cloud ALM Exp...

Hi, Shubham Singh Rao,

Each use case inside of Cloud ALM has separate allowances in how long the data is maintained and the UI enforces the minimum/maximum settings allowed.  If you are unable to set it higher than 14, that is because this function only allows the data to store that long.

In case you would like to have the ability to store it longer, you can add your request/upvote other suggestions already logged in the Influence Campaign:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation   https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Operations           https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Service                 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3669

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
Hello all,   

in case you also come across the issue that you want to log on to HDB of your S/4 and you do not know the passwords this might help in case you are using Eclipse (and legacy HANA Studio)/ DBeaver / VSCode (or generally speaking  ODBC / JDBC) .

Please, use <the container's IP> or localhost if you exposed the port 30213 (i.e. -p 30215:30215) or vhcala4hci if you updated your hosts file.

Finally use Instance 02 and MDC (the port 30215)

* For database SYSTEMDB, use the user SYSTEM with the password ABAPtr2023#00
* For database HDB, use the user SYSTEM with the password ABAPtr2023#00
* For database HDB, use the user SAPA4H with the password ABAPtr2023#00

In my case SAPA4H was locked so I was able to unlock the user and maintain settings for BW Search according to note 2362807 - SAP HANA authorizations for BW metadata search.

@julieplummer20 & Team: Many thanks for providing a on-premise ABAP Plattform for developers.

Br.,

DirkO

entry.title: Re: Bring data from SAP Datasphere to Snowflake
entry.content:
@DC_1 

It is not possible to export data directly to Snowflake using a native Datasphere Replication Flow target. While replication flows support physical data movement to several non-SAP targets, Snowflake is not included in the list of natively supported outbound destinations for this feature.

Instead, the recommended and supported way to integrate SAP Datasphere with Snowflake is through SAP BDC Connect, which uses a zero-copy sharing approach rather than a physical replication flow.

--

KRPK

entry.title: Building a Circular Manufacturing Framework with Nesting Intelligence and SAP
entry.content:
The Manufacturing Crisis
Today’s manufacturers are caught in volatile supply chains and rising material costs on one side, and aggressive sustainability mandates on the other. The traditional "linear" model to buy, make, scrap, and dispose is not only just environmentally irresponsible; it is financially unsustainable. To remain competitive, we must pivot toward a regenerative, circular manufacturing system.

A Unified Framework for Material Intelligence
This white paper introduces a unified framework designed to bridge the gap between physical production and financial transparency. By integrating digital material intelligence, 3PL collaboration, and SAP Product Costing + Material Ledger, we can transform how raw materials are managed.

The Solution: The Nesting Intelligence Sidecar
The centerpiece of this framework is the Nesting Intelligence Sidecar. Rather than cluttering the ERP core with complex geometry calculations, this sidecar operates alongside SAP to provide:

  • Real-Time Nest Yields: Immediate visibility into material utilization before the first cut is made.
  • Automated Remnant Selection: Intelligent identification and tagging of off cuts for future production orders.
  • Optimization Algorithms: Advanced logic that synchronizes directly with SAP Material Consumption to ensure the "Clean Core" remains accurate.

Closing the Loop on the Clean Core
Integrating a Nesting Intelligence Sidecar is more than a technical upgrade; it is the essential link between linear waste and circular efficiency. By feeding real-time yields and remnant data directly into the Material Ledger, manufacturers finally achieve a financial record that reflects the true cost—and the true sustainability—of their operations.

Join the Conversation
How is your organization managing material remnants and off cuts within SAP today? Are you exploring "sidecar" architecture to connect advanced shop-floor optimization with your ERP?

Thanks

Vish Nemani

entry.title: Report for Sales Order Value vs Billing Amount (Open Sales Value) in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a business requirement to create a report showing Billing Amount vs Sales Order Value. The goal is to identify the open (unbilled) sales value at sales order level.

Note: We are using order-related billing and do not create deliveries.

We have already checked standard apps such as:

  • Manage Sales Orders
  • Sales Volume
  • Flexible Analysis

However, we could not find any fields related to billing amount in these apps.

Is there any standard Fiori app or report available to fulfill this requirement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, or is a custom solution required?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Report for Sales Order Value vs Billing Amount (Open...
entry.content:
Hi,

This requirement is currently not available as a standard app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Standard analytical apps such as Sales Volume and Flexible Analysis are primarily based on billing (invoiced) data, whereas apps like Manage Sales Orders focus on sales order data only. Due to this separation, there is no standard report that directly compares Sales Order Value vs Billing Amount to determine open (unbilled) sales value.

In your scenario (order-related billing without deliveries), this behavior is expected, as sales and billing data are exposed through separate analytical models (CDS views) and are not combined in standard content.

To meet this requirement, the recommended approach is to create a Custom CDS View or Custom Analytical Query, where:

Sales order item data is used for ordered value
Billing document data is used for billed amount
Both are linked via the reference between billing documents and sales orders
A calculated field is created to derive the open sales value

If this functionality is important for your business and you would prefer it as part of standard SAP content, you may consider submitting a feature request via KBA

2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

This allows SAP Product Management to evaluate the requirement for future releases.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: API: Read and Modify WBS element Status
entry.content:
Dear @XANDOGAN,

No, customer-defined project/WBS user statuses such as Z001, Z002, etc. cannot be created or maintained as standard configuration in Public Cloud. The classic PS status profile configuration known from on-premise/private cloud, such as transaction OK02 for user status profile definition is not available as a customer configuration option in Public Cloud. SAP confirms that the user status for projects/WBS elements in S/4HANA Cloud cannot be influenced by the user and is used internally by the system to prevent specific business transactions

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2810998

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: AI Agents Are Now a Board-Level Risk. Is Your Governance Ready?
entry.content:
The board conversation about AI has shifted. It used to be about ambition: which use cases to pursue, how quickly to scale, whether the organization was moving fast enough. Increasingly, it's about accountability. What agents are running across the enterprise, what they're costing, whether they're delivering returns, and what happens when something goes wrong. 

Most CIOs are not yet ready for that conversation. 

The reason is structural. AI agents have proliferated faster than governance has followed. Teams across the business have deployed agents independently, on multiple platforms, for multiple purposes, with varying degrees of oversight. The result resembles the early shadow IT problem: ungoverned assets, unclear ownership, untracked costs. But there's a critical difference. Agents act autonomously. They access data, execute decisions, and interact with other systems without a human in the loop. The risk profile is not the same as an unauthorized SaaS subscription. It's considerably higher. 

Three questions define the board-level governance challenge. 

The first is compliance. Regulatory frameworks, including the EU AI Act, require organizations to maintain an inventory of AI systems in use, document associated risks, and demonstrate control. Meeting that bar requires architecture context, documentation workflows, and cross-platform visibility. Most organizations do not yet have the infrastructure to confidently answer an auditor's questions. 

The second is returns. Deployment count is not a measure of value. The organizations that can answer the ROI question are those that have connected their agents to the business processes they were meant to improve and can show whether those processes actually changed. Without that linkage, AI investment is an act of faith. 

The third is control. Knowing which agents are running is the floor, not the ceiling. Control means knowing who owns each agent, what it accesses, whether it is authorized to run, and what the continuity plan is if it fails. It means governance enforced at runtime, not documented after the fact. 

The SAP AI Agent Hub is built to give CIOs confident answers to all three. It provides a single, vendor-agnostic view of the entire agent landscape, grounded in the architecture and business process context needed to move from inventory to accountability. 

The CIOs who will lead on AI are the ones investing in governance infrastructure now, before the problem becomes expensive to fix. That's the lesson shadow IT already taught us.

entry.title: create identity column
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

I have installed 16.1 version of SAP ASE.

Then I started the dbisql and tried the following:

 

CREATE TABLE leagues(id integer identity primary key, name varchar(100), drafttype integer, scoringtype integer, roundvalues integer, leaguetype char(5), salary integer, benchplayers integer);

 

This command gave an error.

 

Then I tried:

 

CREATE TABLE leagues(id int identity primary key, name varchar(100), drafttype integer, scoringtype integer, roundvalues integer, leaguetype char(5), salary integer, benchplayers integer);

 

and that succeeded.

 

Shouldn't the first succeed as well?

 

Then I tried the following:

 

INSERT INTO leagues VALUES(1,'Demo - Roto (Auction)',1,1,1,'demo',260,0);

 

That failed with the explanation that I need to supply the field list.

So then I tried:

 

INSERT INTO leagues(id, name, drafttype, scoringtype, roundvalues, leaguetype, salary, benchplayers) VALUES(1,'Demo - Roto (Auction)',1,1,1,'demo',260,0);

That also failed.

However doing:

 

INSERT INTO leagues(name, drafttype, scoringtype, roundvalues, leaguetype, salary, benchplayers) VALUES('Demo - Roto (Auction)',1,1,1,'demo',260,0);

 

succeeded.

Shouldn't the second INSERT succeed as well?

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How do I create a JDBC data source for an on-premises AS400 (DB2) and which database type Can I
entry.content:
We are currently working on establishing JDBC connectivity between SAP CPI and the DB2 AS400 (IBM iSeries)(On premsie-conecting via cloud connector) system as part of the current integration requirement.

While configuring the JDBC connection, we are encountering connectivity and driver-related issues during connection testing from SAP CPI.

From our initial analysis and online references, we observed that SAP CPI standard JDBC support for DB2 AS400 (IBM i) may have certain limitations compared to standard DB2 LUW databases.

We need confirmation on the following points:

  1. Whether SAP CPI officially supports DB2 AS400 / IBM iSeries connectivity.
  2. Which JDBC driver should be used for this setup.
  3. Whether any additional configuration is required from CPI / BTP / Cloud Connector side.
  4. Whether similar integrations were previously implemented in SAP Integration Suite.
  5. Whether there are any known restrictions or recommended approaches for AS400 connectivity.

Currently, we are testing using the below JDBC URL formats:

  • jdbc:db2://virtual host:virtual port/databasename

Any guidance, documentation, supported approach, or confirmation from SAP would help us proceed further.

Thanks,
Praveen

entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

I installed SQL Anywhere 17 Client package on my Linux box:

 

[code]

igor@IgorReinCloud ~ $ ls -la /opt/sqlanywhere17/
total 244
drwxr-xr-x 18 root root 4096 May 19 00:31 .
drwxr-xr-x 6 root root 4096 May 19 00:30 ..

-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 17160 May 19 00:31 install_20260519-0030.log

drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 May 19 00:30 java

-r--r--r-- 1 root root 9115 May 19 00:31 readme_zh.txt

[/code]

My question is -- how do I identify where the development files are installed?

 

[code]

_=/opt/sqlanywhere17/
